title: Vivarium verdict snapshot (contract v1)
description: Verdict snapshot file shipped alongside Layer 2 (Docker catalogue) and Layer 3 (record-replay catalogue) reproduction pages in the Vivarium gallery. Layer 1 (WASM in-page) reproductions do not ship this file — their verdict is published live in the page DOM and JS globals. See https://aletheia-works.github.io/vivarium/spec/contract-v1 for the full contract.

contract.description: Version literal. Always 'v1' for files validating against this schema; future versions will be siblings (verdict-v2.schema.json) with their own const.

verdict.description: Snapshot verdict. 'pass' means the upstream bug reproduced when this snapshot was captured; 'fail' means it did not (either the runtime shipped a fix or the runtime regressed before producing a verdict). 'pending' is intentionally not allowed in the file form — the snapshot is written post-run.

exit_code.description: Exit code of the recorded program (Layer 3) or the in-container reproducer (Layer 2) at the time of the snapshot. The contract does not constrain which value means 'pass' — that is per-recipe and is interpreted alongside the 'verdict' field above.

image_tag.description: Docker image tag the snapshot was captured against. Typically of the form 'ghcr.io/aletheia-works/vivarium-<slug>:<git-sha>' for CI-pushed images, or '<slug>:dev (local)' for locally-built captures (Layer 3 maintainer-side).

image_digest.description: Docker image identifier at capture time. CI-pushed captures use the registry RepoDigest ('sha256:…'). Layer 3 maintainer-side local-build captures use the local image ID ('sha256:…' from `docker inspect --format='{{.Id}}'`) since RepoDigests are unavailable for never-pushed images. Empty string is allowed when neither is available.

captured_at.description: Wall-clock timestamp of the snapshot capture, ISO-8601 with 'Z' suffix (e.g. '2026-04-27T03:44:39Z').

stdout.description: Full stdout of the recorded run, or the page-specific JSON-encoded output the recipe writes to stdout. Length is unbounded by the schema; pages should keep this reasonable for page-load size.

stderr_tail.description: Last 4 KiB of stderr, truncated from the front when longer. The 4 KiB cap is a CI convention to keep the artifact small; the schema does not enforce a length bound.

examples.stderr_tail: verdict=pass — flock(2) is advisory; cat(1) bypassed the exclusive lock
